---
title: "Active Sonar DSP, Live in Your Browser"
subtitle: "A textbook 10-step chain rendered as an interactive single-file HTML"
author: "DSP Demo"
date: today
engine: markdown
output-file: index.html
---

```{=html}
<div class="build-tag" role="note">
  <span class="build-tag__label">Build</span>
  <code class="build-tag__value">v15</code>
</div>
<style>
  .build-tag {
    font-size: 0.82rem;
    color: #666;
    margin: -0.4rem 0 1.1rem 0;
    display: inline-flex;
    gap: 0.4rem;
    align-items: baseline;
  }
  .build-tag__label {
    text-transform: uppercase;
    letter-spacing: 0.06em;
    font-weight: 600;
  }
  .build-tag__value {
    background: #f0f2f5;
    padding: 1px 7px;
    border-radius: 3px;
    font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace;
    color: #1a1a1a;
  }
</style>
```

```{=html}
<div id="pyo-loader" class="pyo-loader" role="status" aria-live="polite">
  <div class="pyo-loader__spinner" aria-hidden="true"></div>
  <div class="pyo-loader__body">
    <div class="pyo-loader__title">Booting the Python runtime…</div>
    <div class="pyo-loader__stage" id="pyo-loader-stage">Fetching Pyodide + scientific wheels (about 25 MB, cached after first visit).</div>
  </div>
</div>
<style>
  .pyo-loader {
    position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 1000;
    display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.9rem;
    padding: 0.7rem 1rem;
    background: linear-gradient(135deg, #1f5fa8 0%, #2e7ab7 100%);
    color: #fff;
    box-shadow: 0 2px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.15);
    font-size: 0.92rem;
    transition: opacity 0.6s ease, transform 0.6s ease;
  }
  .pyo-loader__spinner {
    width: 20px; height: 20px;
    border: 3px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.35);
    border-top-color: #fff;
    border-radius: 50%;
    animation: pyo-spin 0.8s linear infinite;
    flex: 0 0 auto;
  }
  .pyo-loader__title { font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.2; }
  .pyo-loader__stage { font-size: 0.82rem; opacity: 0.92; margin-top: 0.1rem; }
  .pyo-loader.is-ready { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #2d8a4b 0%, #3aa560 100%); }
  .pyo-loader.is-ready .pyo-loader__spinner { border: 3px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.8); animation: none; }
  .pyo-loader.is-hidden { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(-100%); pointer-events: none; }
  @keyframes pyo-spin { to { transform: rotate(360deg); } }
</style>
<script>
// Rewrite the engine-url to an absolute URL *before* quarto-live's OJS
// setup reads the `pyodide-data` blob. quarto-live spawns the Pyodide
// worker from a Blob URL, so a relative `./pyodide/` path resolves
// against the unusable blob base and the runtime never loads. Running
// on DOMContentLoaded means the data tag is already parsed.
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function () {
  try {
    const tag = document.querySelector('script[type="pyodide-data"]');
    if (!tag) return;
    const b64 = tag.textContent.trim();
    const data = JSON.parse(atob(b64));
    const idx = data && data.options && data.options.indexURL;
    if (idx && !/^[a-z]+:\/\//i.test(idx) && !idx.startsWith('/')) {
      data.options.indexURL = new URL(idx, document.baseURI).href;
      tag.textContent = btoa(JSON.stringify(data));
      console.log('[pyo-loader] indexURL rewritten to', data.options.indexURL);
    }
  } catch (e) {
    console.error('[pyo-loader] engine-url rewrite failed', e);
  }
});

(function () {
  const el = document.getElementById('pyo-loader');
  const stage = document.getElementById('pyo-loader-stage');
  const fallback = [
    [0,  'Fetching Pyodide runtime (same-origin, no CDN)…'],
    [8,  'Loading NumPy / SciPy / Matplotlib wheels…'],
    [20, 'Mounting the sonar package…'],
    [40, 'Still booting — slow network or cold cache. Check DevTools → Network.'],
  ];
  let t = 0;
  const tick = setInterval(function () {
    t += 1;
    // Pyodide cells run in a Worker (no `window`), so we can't set a
    // readiness flag from Python. Instead, watch for the setup cell's
    // print output to appear in its container on the main thread.
    if (!window.sonarReady) {
      const cell1 = document.getElementById('pyodide-1');
      if (cell1 && /Sonar package loaded successfully/.test(cell1.textContent || '')) {
        window.sonarReady = true;
      }
    }
    if (window.sonarReady) {
      clearInterval(tick);
      el.classList.add('is-ready');
      const title = el.querySelector('.pyo-loader__title');
      if (title) title.textContent = 'Loaded successfully';
      stage.textContent = 'Drag the sliders — figures redraw automatically.';
      // Don't hold the banner: fade after a brief confirmation window.
      setTimeout(function () { el.classList.add('is-hidden'); }, 2800);
      setTimeout(function () { if (el.parentNode) el.parentNode.removeChild(el); }, 3800);
      return;
    }
    // Prefer quarto-live's own status text while Pyodide is still booting.
    const live = document.getElementById('exercise-loading-status');
    const liveText = live && live.textContent && live.textContent.trim();
    if (liveText) {
      // Rephrase "Downloading X" -> "Loading X" for the reader.
      stage.textContent = liveText.replace(/^Downloading\b/i, 'Loading');
      return;
    }
    // quarto-live removes that element once Pyodide init is complete.
    // If sonarReady hasn't fired yet, it means the first {pyodide} cell
    // is still evaluating (or errored).
    if (!live) {
      stage.textContent = 'Pyodide ready — running the first Python cell…';
      return;
    }
    for (let i = fallback.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
      if (t >= fallback[i][0]) { stage.textContent = fallback[i][1]; break; }
    }
  }, 1000);
})();
</script>
```

## Abstract {.unnumbered}

The DSP here is intentionally textbook — an LFM chirp, two-way spreading,
matched filtering, range/Doppler, CA-CFAR. **The point of this document is
not the DSP. The point is that everything below is running in your browser**:
no server, no Jupyter kernel, no cloud function. The numerical code is real
NumPy/SciPy executing inside Pyodide; the controls are wired through OJS
reactivity; the figures redraw on each knob change in well under a second.
The whole experience ships as a single self-contained `report.html` you can
host on any static URL — or open from your local filesystem.

::: {.callout-tip appearance="minimal"}
**First load.** Pyodide and its scientific stack are about 10 MB on first
visit. Grab a coffee, then everything thereafter is local.
:::

## Setup {.unnumbered}

The first cell loads the runtime, mounts the `sonar/` Python package
(inlined into this HTML at render time), and primes the chain. It runs
once, on first cell evaluation. Every later cell does `import sonar` like
any normal CPython script — the *same* code that the `pytest` suite runs.

```{pyodide}
#| autorun: true
#| edit: false
#| echo: false
import sys

# quarto-live's `pyodide.resources: [sonar]` fetches sonar/*.py on the
# main thread and writes them into the Pyodide worker's VFS at paths
# relative to cwd (/home/pyodide). Add that to sys.path so `import sonar`
# finds the package we just mounted.
if "/home/pyodide" not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, "/home/pyodide")

import numpy as np
# Don't force a matplotlib backend here: quarto-live installs its own
# display hook (matplotlib_display.py) during Pyodide setup that
# intercepts plt.show() and injects the figure into the cell's output.
# Pinning AGG breaks that and triggers the
# "FigureCanvasAgg is non-interactive" warning.
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import sonar
print("Sonar package loaded successfully.")
```

::: {#controls-dock}

## Controls

```{=html}
<div id="calc-status" class="calc-status calc-status--idle">
  <span class="calc-status__dot" aria-hidden="true"></span>
  <span class="calc-status__text">Waiting for first run…</span>
</div>
<style>
  .calc-status {
    display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem;
    padding: 0.4rem 0.5rem;
    margin-bottom: 0.6rem;
    border-radius: 6px;
    font-size: 0.82rem;
    border: 1px solid transparent;
    transition: background 0.2s, border-color 0.2s, color 0.2s;
  }
  .calc-status__dot {
    width: 10px; height: 10px; border-radius: 50%;
    background: currentColor;
    flex: 0 0 auto;
    box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.04);
  }
  .calc-status--idle {
    color: #6b7280; background: #f3f4f6; border-color: #e5e7eb;
  }
  .calc-status--running {
    color: #b45309; background: #fef3c7; border-color: #fcd34d;
  }
  .calc-status--running .calc-status__dot {
    animation: calc-pulse 0.9s ease-in-out infinite;
  }
  .calc-status--done {
    color: #065f46; background: #d1fae5; border-color: #a7f3d0;
  }
  @keyframes calc-pulse {
    0%, 100% { transform: scale(1); opacity: 1; }
    50%      { transform: scale(1.4); opacity: 0.55; }
  }
</style>
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Derive a *committed* reactive value from a live Inputs.range view.
// The view itself still emits on every drag pixel (so on-screen labels
// and derived readouts update fluently), while the committed generator
// only yields on 'change' (mouse release / arrow-key release), so
// expensive pyodide cells downstream only fire once per gesture.
committed = (view) => {
  const el = (view.querySelector && view.querySelector("input[type=range]")) || view;
  const read = () => (el.valueAsNumber !== undefined ? el.valueAsNumber : el.value);
  return Generators.observe((next) => {
    next(read());
    const h = () => next(read());
    el.addEventListener("change", h);
    return () => el.removeEventListener("change", h);
  });
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof B_live = Inputs.range([100, 10000], {value: 4000, step: 100, label: "B (Hz)"})
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
B = committed(viewof B_live)
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
md`Δr = c/(2B) = **${(1500/(2*B_live)).toFixed(2)} m**`
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof tau_live = Inputs.range([0.01, 0.5], {value: 0.05, step: 0.005, label: "τ (s)"})
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
tau = committed(viewof tau_live)
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
md`Bτ = **${(B_live*tau_live).toFixed(0)}**, MF gain = **${(10*Math.log10(B_live*tau_live)).toFixed(1)} dB**`
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof R0_live = Inputs.range([100, 5000], {value: 1500, step: 25, label: "R₀ (m)"})
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
R0 = committed(viewof R0_live)
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof v_live = Inputs.range([-20, 20], {value: 5, step: 0.5, label: "v (m/s)"})
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
v = committed(viewof v_live)
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof snr_in_dB_live = Inputs.range([-20, 20], {value: -5, step: 1, label: "SNR (dB)"})
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
snr_in_dB = committed(viewof snr_in_dB_live)
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof fc_live = Inputs.range([5000, 50000], {value: 20000, step: 1000, label: "fc (Hz)"})
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
fc = committed(viewof fc_live)
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof PRI_live = Inputs.range([0.0005, 0.02], {value: 0.002, step: 0.0005, label: "PRI (s)"})
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
PRI = committed(viewof PRI_live)
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
N = 16
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
md`PRI = **${(PRI_live*1000).toFixed(2)} ms**, Δv = **${(1500/fc_live/(2*N*PRI_live)).toFixed(2)} m/s** (N=${N})`
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof N_pulse = Inputs.select([1, 4, 16, 64], {value: 16, label: "N_pulse"})
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof Pfa_log_live = Inputs.range([-6, -2], {value: -4, step: 0.5, label: "log₁₀ Pfa"})
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Pfa_log = committed(viewof Pfa_log_live)
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Pfa = Math.pow(10, Pfa_log)
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
md`Design Pfa = **${Math.pow(10, Pfa_log_live).toExponential(1)}**`
```
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof apply_mf = Inputs.toggle({value: true, label: "Apply matched filter"})
```

:::

```{=html}
<script src="./plotly/plotly-cartesian.min.js"></script>
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// pyRun: serialised Python execution tied to the calc-status pill.
// Run a block of Python where the last expression must be a JSON
// string; named OJS values are injected into Pyodide globals and the
// parsed JSON is returned. All calls queue onto a single promise so
// globals.set() cannot race between concurrent pyRun invocations.
pyRun = {
  let queue = Promise.resolve();
  const statusEl = document.getElementById('calc-status');
  const textEl = statusEl && statusEl.querySelector('.calc-status__text');
  let pending = 0, total = 0, done = 0;
  const setStatus = (cls, msg) => {
    if (!statusEl || !textEl) return;
    statusEl.classList.remove('calc-status--idle', 'calc-status--running', 'calc-status--done');
    statusEl.classList.add('calc-status--' + cls);
    textEl.textContent = msg;
  };
  const onStart = () => {
    if (pending === 0) { total = 0; done = 0; }
    pending += 1; total += 1;
    setStatus('running', `Running… (${done}/${total} plots)`);
  };
  const onFinish = (ok) => {
    pending = Math.max(0, pending - 1);
    if (ok) done += 1;
    if (pending === 0) setStatus('done', `Complete (${done}/${total} plots)`);
    else setStatus('running', `Running… (${done}/${total} plots)`);
  };
  return (code, vars) => {
    onStart();
    const next = queue.then(async () => {
      const p = await pyodideOjs.pyodidePromise;
      for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(vars || {})) p.globals.set(k, v);
      return p.runPythonAsync(code);
    });
    queue = next.catch(() => {});  // keep queue alive on error
    return next.then(
      (r) => { onFinish(true); return typeof r === 'string' ? JSON.parse(r) : r; },
      (e) => { onFinish(false); throw e; }
    );
  };
}
```


```{=html}
<style>
  /* Sticky right-side controls dock on wide screens. On narrow screens
     the panel reverts to inline flow. */
  #controls-dock {
    font-size: 0.86rem;
  }
  #controls-dock h2 {
    margin-top: 0;
    font-size: 1rem;
    font-weight: 600;
    border-bottom: 1px solid #e4e7ec;
    padding-bottom: 0.3rem;
    margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
  }
  #controls-dock .observablehq--inspect,
  #controls-dock form {
    margin-bottom: 0.25rem;
  }
  @media (min-width: 1200px) {
    main.content, main#quarto-document-content {
      max-width: none;
    }
    #controls-dock {
      position: fixed;
      top: 1rem;
      right: 1rem;
      width: 320px;
      max-height: calc(100vh - 2rem);
      overflow-y: auto;
      background: #fff;
      border: 1px solid #e2e5eb;
      border-radius: 8px;
      padding: 0.7rem 1rem;
      box-shadow: 0 2px 12px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.06);
      z-index: 50;
    }
    /* reserve the right gutter so main content doesn't slide under the panel */
    body { padding-right: 360px; }
  }
</style>
```


## Step 1 — Transmit waveform

A linear-FM (chirp) pulse at complex baseband:

$$
s(t) = \exp\!\left(j\pi\frac{B}{\tau}\,t^2\right), \qquad t\in[0,\tau).
$$ {#eq-chirp}

Instantaneous frequency sweeps linearly from 0 to $B$ over the pulse.
The time–bandwidth product $B\tau$ is the only knob that matters here —
it sets every downstream resolution number.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step1_data = await pyRun(`
import json, numpy as np
from sonar.waveform import lfm_chirp
from scipy.signal import spectrogram
Fs = max(4 * B, 2 * fc)
t, s = lfm_chirp(B, tau, Fs)
# downsample waveform for plotting
step = max(1, s.size // 4000)
tt_wave = (t[::step] * 1e3).tolist()
re_wave = s.real[::step].tolist()
nperseg = min(256, max(32, s.size // 16))
f_spec, t_spec, Sxx = spectrogram(s.real, Fs, nperseg=nperseg, noverlap=nperseg // 2)
Sxx_dB = 10.0 * np.log10(Sxx + 1e-12)
json.dumps({
  "tt_wave": tt_wave, "re_wave": re_wave,
  "spec_t_ms": (t_spec * 1e3).tolist(),
  "spec_f_kHz": (f_spec / 1e3).tolist(),
  "spec_dB": Sxx_dB.tolist(),
  "Bt": float(B * tau),
})
`, {B, tau, fc})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step1_waveform = Plot.plot({
  width: 720, height: 200,
  marginLeft: 55, marginBottom: 38,
  x: {label: "time (ms)", grid: true},
  y: {label: "Re s(t)", grid: true},
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([0], {stroke: "#ddd"}),
    Plot.line(step1_data.tt_wave.map((t, i) => ({t, y: step1_data.re_wave[i]})),
              {x: "t", y: "y", stroke: "steelblue", strokeWidth: 0.7}),
  ],
  caption: `LFM chirp, Bτ = ${step1_data.Bt.toFixed(0)}`,
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step1_spec = {
  const d = step1_data;
  const div = html`<div style="width:100%;height:240px"></div>`;
  const zmax = Math.max(...d.spec_dB.flat());
  Plotly.newPlot(div, [{
    type: "heatmap",
    z: d.spec_dB, x: d.spec_t_ms, y: d.spec_f_kHz,
    colorscale: "Viridis", zmin: zmax - 50, zmax: zmax,
    colorbar: {title: "dB", thickness: 10},
    hovertemplate: "t=%{x:.2f} ms<br>f=%{y:.2f} kHz<br>%{z:.1f} dB<extra></extra>",
  }], {
    margin: {l: 55, r: 10, t: 30, b: 38},
    xaxis: {title: "time (ms)"},
    yaxis: {title: "freq (kHz)"},
    title: {text: "spectrogram of Re s(t)", font: {size: 13}},
  }, {displayModeBar: false, responsive: true});
  return div;
}
```

## Step 2 — Two-way propagation loss

Spherical spreading + Thorp absorption gives one-way TL of
$\mathrm{TL}(R, f) = 20\log_{10}R + \alpha(f)\,R\cdot 10^{-3}$ dB,
with Thorp's coefficient $\alpha$ in dB/km. The two-way loss is just $2\,\mathrm{TL}$.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step2_data = await pyRun(`
import json, numpy as np
from sonar.propagation import transmission_loss, two_way_loss, thorp_absorption
R = np.logspace(1, 4, 400)
json.dumps({
  "R": R.tolist(),
  "one_way": transmission_loss(R, fc).tolist(),
  "two_way": two_way_loss(R, fc).tolist(),
  "alpha": float(thorp_absorption(fc)),
  "fc_kHz": float(fc) / 1e3,
})
`, {fc})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step2_plot = {
  const d = step2_data;
  const rows = d.R.flatMap((R, i) => [
    {R, loss: d.one_way[i], kind: "one-way TL"},
    {R, loss: d.two_way[i], kind: "two-way 2·TL"},
  ]);
  return Plot.plot({
    width: 720, height: 240,
    marginLeft: 55, marginBottom: 38,
    x: {label: "range (m)", type: "log", grid: true},
    y: {label: "loss (dB)", grid: true},
    color: {legend: true, domain: ["one-way TL", "two-way 2·TL"]},
    marks: [Plot.line(rows, {x: "R", y: "loss", stroke: "kind", strokeWidth: 1.3})],
    caption: `propagation loss at fc = ${d.fc_kHz.toFixed(1)} kHz, α = ${d.alpha.toFixed(2)} dB/km`,
  });
}
```

## Step 3 — Target echo

A point target at range $R_0$ moving with radial velocity $v$ returns a
copy of the transmit waveform delayed by $\tau_d = 2R_0/c$ and shifted in
frequency by the (narrow-band) Doppler $f_d = -2vf_c/c$. Amplitude is set
by the radar equation in dB,

$$
\text{level} = \text{SL} - 2\,\mathrm{TL}(R_0, f_c) + \text{TS}.
$$ {#eq-radareqn}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step3_data = await pyRun(`
import json, numpy as np
from sonar.waveform import lfm_chirp
from sonar.targets import Target, echo
from sonar import C_WATER
Fs = max(4 * B, 2 * fc)
_, s = lfm_chirp(B, tau, Fs)
tgt = Target(R0_m=R0, v_mps=v, TS_dB=-15.0)
y = echo(s, Fs, fc, tgt, SL_dB=200.0)
t = np.arange(y.size) / Fs
step = max(1, y.size // 4000)
json.dumps({
  "t": t[::step].tolist(), "y_real": y.real[::step].tolist(),
  "tau_d": float(2 * R0 / C_WATER),
  "R0": float(R0), "v": float(v),
})
`, {B, tau, fc, R0, v})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step3_plot = {
  const d = step3_data;
  const pts = d.t.map((t, i) => ({t, y: d.y_real[i]}));
  return Plot.plot({
    width: 720, height: 230,
    marginLeft: 60, marginBottom: 38,
    x: {label: "time since transmit (s)", grid: true},
    y: {label: "Re y(t)", grid: true},
    marks: [
      Plot.ruleY([0], {stroke: "#ddd"}),
      Plot.line(pts, {x: "t", y: "y", stroke: "steelblue", strokeWidth: 0.7}),
      Plot.ruleX([d.tau_d], {stroke: "#c0392b", strokeDasharray: "4 3"}),
      Plot.text([{x: d.tau_d, y: 0}],
        {x: "x", y: "y", text: [`τ_d = ${(d.tau_d*1e3).toFixed(1)} ms`],
         fill: "#c0392b", dx: 4, dy: -6, textAnchor: "start", fontSize: 10}),
    ],
    caption: `echo from target at ${d.R0.toFixed(0)} m, v = ${d.v.toFixed(1)} m/s`,
  });
}
```

## Step 4 — Reverberation

Diffuse bottom/volume scatterers produce a complex-Gaussian process with an
exponentially decaying envelope, bandlimited to the chirp band. We don't
attempt physical levels — just enough structure to put a realistic
clutter floor under the matched-filter peak.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step4_data = await pyRun(`
import json, numpy as np
from sonar.reverberation import reverberation
from sonar import C_WATER
Fs = max(4 * B, 2 * fc)
n = min(int(2.5 * 2 * R0 / C_WATER * Fs) + 8192, 16384)
rng = np.random.default_rng(0)
r = reverberation(n, Fs, B, decay_tau_s=0.4, rev_level=1.0, rng=rng)
t = np.arange(n) / Fs
step = max(1, n // 4000)
json.dumps({
  "t": t[::step].tolist(),
  "env": np.abs(r)[::step].tolist(),
  "B": float(B),
})
`, {B, tau, fc, R0})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step4_plot = {
  const d = step4_data;
  const pts = d.t.map((t, i) => ({t, y: d.env[i]}));
  return Plot.plot({
    width: 720, height: 220,
    marginLeft: 55, marginBottom: 38,
    x: {label: "time (s)", grid: true},
    y: {label: "|reverb|", grid: true},
    marks: [Plot.line(pts, {x: "t", y: "y", stroke: "#555", strokeWidth: 0.5})],
    caption: `reverberation envelope, B = ${d.B.toFixed(0)} Hz`,
  });
}
```

## Step 5 — Additive noise + composite received signal

Round it out with white complex-Gaussian noise. The total received signal
is $x(t) = y_\text{echo}(t) + r(t) + n(t)$, with the noise level set so
that the input SNR (relative to the echo) matches the slider.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step5_data = await pyRun(`
import json, numpy as np
from sonar.waveform import lfm_chirp
from sonar.targets import Target, echo
from sonar.reverberation import reverberation
from sonar.noise import white_noise, noise_sigma_for_snr
from sonar import C_WATER
Fs = max(4 * B, 2 * fc)
_, s = lfm_chirp(B, tau, Fs)
n_total = min(int(2.0 * R0 / C_WATER * Fs) + 8192, 16384)
tgt = Target(R0_m=R0, v_mps=v, TS_dB=-15.0)
y_e = echo(s, Fs, fc, tgt, SL_dB=200.0, n_out=n_total)
rng = np.random.default_rng(1)
y_r = reverberation(n_total, Fs, B, decay_tau_s=0.2, rev_level=1e8, rng=rng)
sig_pwr = np.mean(np.abs(y_e[y_e != 0]) ** 2) if np.any(y_e != 0) else 1.0
y_n = white_noise(n_total, noise_sigma_for_snr(sig_pwr, snr_in_dB), rng=rng)
x = y_e + y_r + y_n
w = np.hanning(n_total)
def psd(z):
    Z = np.fft.fft(z * w)
    return np.abs(Z) ** 2 / (Fs * (w * w).sum())
f = np.fft.fftshift(np.fft.fftfreq(n_total, 1.0 / Fs))
Pxx = np.fft.fftshift(psd(x))
Pee = np.fft.fftshift(psd(y_e))
Prr = np.fft.fftshift(psd(y_r))
Pnn = np.fft.fftshift(psd(y_n))
# downsample PSD for plotting
step = max(1, f.size // 2048)
def dB(p): return (10*np.log10(p + 1e-30))[::step].tolist()
json.dumps({
  "f_kHz": (f[::step] / 1e3).tolist(),
  "x": dB(Pxx), "e": dB(Pee), "r": dB(Prr), "n": dB(Pnn),
  "snr_in_dB": float(snr_in_dB),
  "Fs_kHz": float(Fs) / 1e3,
})
`, {B, tau, fc, R0, v, snr_in_dB})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step5_plot = {
  const d = step5_data;
  const series = [
    ["received x",  d.x, "#1f77b4"],
    ["echo only",   d.e, "#2ca02c"],
    ["reverb only", d.r, "#ff7f0e"],
    ["noise only",  d.n, "#9467bd"],
  ];
  const rows = series.flatMap(([name, arr]) =>
    d.f_kHz.map((f, i) => ({f, dB: arr[i], series: name}))
  );
  return Plot.plot({
    width: 720, height: 280,
    marginLeft: 55, marginBottom: 38,
    x: {label: "baseband freq (kHz)", grid: true, domain: [-d.Fs_kHz/2, d.Fs_kHz/2]},
    y: {label: "PSD (dB)", grid: true},
    color: {legend: true, domain: series.map(s => s[0]), range: series.map(s => s[2])},
    marks: [Plot.line(rows, {x: "f", y: "dB", stroke: "series", strokeWidth: 1})],
    caption: `received PSD, input SNR = ${d.snr_in_dB.toFixed(0)} dB`,
  });
}
```

## Step 6 — Receive conditioning

In hardware: bandpass to the chirp band, then quadrature-demodulate to
complex baseband. Our chain is *already* at complex baseband, so the
"bandpass" here is a baseband lowpass at $\pm B/2$. The PSD before/after
shows the white noise tails getting cut.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step6_data = await pyRun(`
import json, numpy as np
from sonar.waveform import lfm_chirp
from sonar.targets import Target, echo
from sonar.reverberation import reverberation
from sonar.noise import white_noise, noise_sigma_for_snr
from sonar.receive import baseband_filter
from sonar import C_WATER
Fs = max(4 * B, 2 * fc)
_, s = lfm_chirp(B, tau, Fs)
n_total = min(int(2.0 * R0 / C_WATER * Fs) + 8192, 16384)
tgt = Target(R0_m=R0, v_mps=0.0, TS_dB=-15.0)
y_e = echo(s, Fs, fc, tgt, SL_dB=200.0, n_out=n_total)
rng = np.random.default_rng(2)
y_r = reverberation(n_total, Fs, B, decay_tau_s=0.2, rev_level=1e8, rng=rng)
sig_pwr = np.mean(np.abs(y_e[y_e != 0]) ** 2) if np.any(y_e != 0) else 1.0
y_n = white_noise(n_total, noise_sigma_for_snr(sig_pwr, snr_in_dB), rng=rng)
x = y_e + y_r + y_n
x_bb = baseband_filter(x, Fs, B)
w = np.hanning(n_total)
def psd(z):
    Z = np.fft.fft(z * w)
    return np.abs(Z) ** 2 / (Fs * (w * w).sum())
f = np.fft.fftshift(np.fft.fftfreq(n_total, 1.0 / Fs))
step = max(1, f.size // 2048)
def dB(p): return (10*np.log10(np.fft.fftshift(p) + 1e-30))[::step].tolist()
json.dumps({
  "f_kHz": (f[::step] / 1e3).tolist(),
  "before": dB(psd(x)), "after": dB(psd(x_bb)),
  "band_kHz": float(B) / 2e3,
  "Fs_kHz": float(Fs) / 1e3,
})
`, {B, tau, fc, R0, snr_in_dB})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step6_plot = {
  const d = step6_data;
  const rows = d.f_kHz.flatMap((f, i) => [
    {f, dB: d.before[i], panel: "before bandpass"},
    {f, dB: d.after[i],  panel: "after baseband filter"},
  ]);
  return Plot.plot({
    width: 720, height: 260,
    marginLeft: 55, marginBottom: 38,
    x: {label: "baseband freq (kHz)", grid: true, domain: [-d.Fs_kHz/2, d.Fs_kHz/2]},
    y: {label: "PSD (dB)", grid: true},
    fx: {label: null},
    marks: [
      Plot.line(rows, {x: "f", y: "dB", fx: "panel", stroke: "steelblue"}),
      Plot.ruleX([-d.band_kHz, d.band_kHz], {stroke: "#c0392b",
        strokeDasharray: "3 3", strokeOpacity: 0.6}),
    ],
    facet: {data: rows, x: "panel"},
  });
}
```

## Step 7 — Matched filter {.scrollable}

The centerpiece. Cross-correlate the conditioned signal with the
time-reversed conjugate of the transmit pulse. Output peak SNR rises by
$10\log_{10}(B\tau)$ — the only "gain" you get for free in DSP.

::: {.callout-important appearance="simple"}
**Payoff 1.** Toggle **Apply matched filter** in the Controls panel and
watch the target peak go from buried-in-noise to unmissable. The plot
below uses your current SNR slider; flip the toggle to compare bypass vs.
MF on the same axes.
:::

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Compute BOTH bypass and matched-filter outputs in one Python pass.
// The apply_mf toggle is then a JS-only choice — no pyodide round-trip.
step7_data = await pyRun(`
import json, numpy as np
from sonar.waveform import lfm_chirp
from sonar.targets import Target, echo
from sonar.reverberation import reverberation
from sonar.noise import white_noise, noise_sigma_for_snr
from sonar.receive import baseband_filter
from sonar.matched_filter import matched_filter, mf_gain_dB
from sonar import C_WATER
Fs = max(4 * B, 2 * fc)
_, s = lfm_chirp(B, tau, Fs)
n_total = int(2.5 * 2 * R0 / C_WATER * Fs) + 8192
tgt = Target(R0_m=R0, v_mps=v, TS_dB=-15.0)
y_e = echo(s, Fs, fc, tgt, SL_dB=200.0, n_out=n_total)
rng = np.random.default_rng(3)
y_r = reverberation(n_total, Fs, B, decay_tau_s=0.4, rev_level=1e8, rng=rng)
sig_pwr = np.mean(np.abs(y_e[y_e != 0]) ** 2) if np.any(y_e != 0) else 1.0
y_n = white_noise(n_total, noise_sigma_for_snr(sig_pwr, snr_in_dB), rng=rng)
x_bb = baseband_filter(y_e + y_r + y_n, Fs, B)
mf  = matched_filter(x_bb, s)
range_axis = np.arange(n_total) / Fs * C_WATER / 2.0
def norm_dB(z):
    m = 20 * np.log10(np.abs(z) + 1e-12)
    return (m - m.max()).astype(float)
bp_dB, mf_dB = norm_dB(x_bb), norm_dB(mf)
# Restrict to the display window, then down-sample
r_max = 1.2 * R0 + 200
keep = range_axis <= r_max
range_axis = range_axis[keep]
bp_dB, mf_dB = bp_dB[keep], mf_dB[keep]
step = max(1, range_axis.size // 4000)
json.dumps({
  "range": range_axis[::step].tolist(),
  "bypass": bp_dB[::step].tolist(),
  "mf": mf_dB[::step].tolist(),
  "R0": float(R0),
  "mf_gain_dB": float(mf_gain_dB(B, tau)),
})
`, {B, tau, fc, R0, v, snr_in_dB})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Re-renders on apply_mf (JS-side toggle) WITHOUT re-running Python.
step7_plot = {
  const d = step7_data;
  const trace = apply_mf ? d.mf : d.bypass;
  const pts = d.range.map((r, i) => ({r, dB: trace[i]}));
  return Plot.plot({
    width: 720, height: 260,
    marginLeft: 55, marginBottom: 38,
    x: {label: "range (m)", grid: true, domain: [0, d.range[d.range.length-1]]},
    y: {label: "normalised magnitude (dB)", grid: true, domain: [-60, 5]},
    marks: [
      Plot.line(pts, {x: "r", y: "dB", stroke: apply_mf ? "steelblue" : "#888", strokeWidth: 0.7}),
      Plot.ruleX([d.R0], {stroke: "#c0392b", strokeDasharray: "4 3"}),
      Plot.text([{x: d.R0, y: 5}],
        {x: "x", y: "y", text: [`true target @ ${d.R0.toFixed(0)} m`],
         fill: "#c0392b", dx: 4, dy: 0, textAnchor: "start", fontSize: 10}),
    ],
    caption: apply_mf
      ? `matched-filter output (gain ≈ ${d.mf_gain_dB.toFixed(1)} dB)`
      : "BYPASS — raw conditioned signal (no matched filter)",
  });
}
```

### Bandwidth → range resolution {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-important appearance="simple"}
**Payoff 2.** Two equal-strength targets sit 5 m apart, hard-coded for
this demo. Slide **Bandwidth** in the Controls panel from 100 Hz upward;
watch the two MF peaks merge into one blob at low B and split apart as B
grows. The live $\Delta r = c/(2B)$ readout next to the slider tracks
the actual resolution.
:::

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step7b_data = await pyRun(`
import json, numpy as np
from sonar.waveform import lfm_chirp
from sonar.targets import Target, echo
from sonar.matched_filter import matched_filter
from sonar import C_WATER
Fs = max(4 * B, 2 * fc)
_, s = lfm_chirp(B, tau, Fs)
t1 = Target(R0_m=R0,       v_mps=0.0, TS_dB=-15.0)
t2 = Target(R0_m=R0 + 5.0, v_mps=0.0, TS_dB=-15.0)
n_total = int(2.5 * 2 * (R0 + 50) / C_WATER * Fs) + 8192
y = echo(s, Fs, fc, t1, n_out=n_total) + echo(s, Fs, fc, t2, n_out=n_total)
mf = matched_filter(y, s)
range_axis = np.arange(mf.size) / Fs * C_WATER / 2.0
mag = np.abs(mf); mag = mag / mag.max()
# Restrict to the display window
lo, hi = R0 - 30, R0 + 35
keep = (range_axis >= lo) & (range_axis <= hi)
range_axis = range_axis[keep]
mag_dB = 20 * np.log10(mag[keep] + 1e-9)
step = max(1, range_axis.size // 3000)
json.dumps({
  "range": range_axis[::step].tolist(),
  "mag_dB": mag_dB[::step].tolist(),
  "R0": float(R0), "dr": float(C_WATER / (2 * B)),
  "xmin": float(lo), "xmax": float(hi),
})
`, {B, tau, fc, R0})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step7b_plot = {
  const d = step7b_data;
  const pts = d.range.map((r, i) => ({r, dB: d.mag_dB[i]}));
  return Plot.plot({
    width: 720, height: 240,
    marginLeft: 55, marginBottom: 38,
    x: {label: "range (m)", grid: true, domain: [d.xmin, d.xmax]},
    y: {label: "MF mag (dB)", grid: true, domain: [-40, 5]},
    marks: [
      Plot.line(pts, {x: "r", y: "dB", stroke: "steelblue", strokeWidth: 0.8}),
      Plot.ruleX([d.R0],       {stroke: "#c0392b", strokeDasharray: "4 3"}),
      Plot.ruleX([d.R0 + 5.0], {stroke: "#9c27b0", strokeDasharray: "4 3"}),
    ],
    caption: `two targets, separation 5 m  |  Δr = c/(2B) = ${d.dr.toFixed(2)} m`,
  });
}
```

## Step 8 — Envelope detection + TVG

The display chain takes $|y|$, converts to dB, and adds a time-varying
gain that compensates for one-way spreading along the range axis. The
result is what an A-scan would look like on a real sonar console.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step8_data = await pyRun(`
import json, numpy as np
from sonar.waveform import lfm_chirp
from sonar.targets import Target, echo
from sonar.reverberation import reverberation
from sonar.noise import white_noise, noise_sigma_for_snr
from sonar.receive import baseband_filter
from sonar.matched_filter import matched_filter
from sonar.envelope import envelope_dB, tvg
from sonar import C_WATER
Fs = max(4 * B, 2 * fc)
_, s = lfm_chirp(B, tau, Fs)
n_total = int(2.5 * 2 * R0 / C_WATER * Fs) + 8192
tgt = Target(R0_m=R0, v_mps=v, TS_dB=-15.0)
y_e = echo(s, Fs, fc, tgt, SL_dB=200.0, n_out=n_total)
rng = np.random.default_rng(4)
y_r = reverberation(n_total, Fs, B, decay_tau_s=0.4, rev_level=1e8, rng=rng)
sig_pwr = np.mean(np.abs(y_e[y_e != 0]) ** 2) if np.any(y_e != 0) else 1.0
y_n = white_noise(n_total, noise_sigma_for_snr(sig_pwr, snr_in_dB), rng=rng)
mf = matched_filter(baseband_filter(y_e + y_r + y_n, Fs, B), s)
env = envelope_dB(mf)
env_tvg = tvg(env, Fs, fc)
range_axis = np.arange(env.size) / Fs * C_WATER / 2.0
r_max = 1.2 * R0 + 200
keep = range_axis <= r_max
range_axis = range_axis[keep]; env = env[keep]; env_tvg = env_tvg[keep]
step = max(1, range_axis.size // 4000)
json.dumps({
  "range": range_axis[::step].tolist(),
  "env": env[::step].tolist(),
  "env_tvg": env_tvg[::step].tolist(),
  "R0": float(R0), "r_max": float(r_max),
})
`, {B, tau, fc, R0, v, snr_in_dB})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step8_plot = {
  const d = step8_data;
  const rows = d.range.flatMap((r, i) => [
    {r, dB: d.env[i],     series: "envelope (dB)"},
    {r, dB: d.env_tvg[i], series: "envelope + TVG (dB)"},
  ]);
  return Plot.plot({
    width: 720, height: 240,
    marginLeft: 55, marginBottom: 38,
    x: {label: "range (m)", grid: true, domain: [0, d.r_max]},
    y: {label: "level (dB rel.)", grid: true},
    color: {legend: true, domain: ["envelope (dB)", "envelope + TVG (dB)"]},
    marks: [
      Plot.line(rows, {x: "r", y: "dB", stroke: "series", strokeWidth: 0.8}),
      Plot.ruleX([d.R0], {stroke: "#c0392b", strokeDasharray: "4 3"}),
    ],
    caption: `post-MF envelope, with and without TVG; target @ ${d.R0.toFixed(0)} m`,
  });
}
```

## Step 9 — Range–Doppler map

Stack $N_\text{pulse}$ consecutive pings along a slow-time axis, FFT
across that axis, and you get a range–Doppler image. Coherent
integration gain is $10\log_{10}N_\text{pulse}$ — a clean 18 dB at $N=64$
versus $N=1$.

::: {.callout-important appearance="simple"}
**Payoff 3.** Set **Pulses integrated** in Controls to 1, then 4, 16, 64.
The target cell rises while its position stays fixed; the noise floor
stays put. That's coherent gain in real time.
:::

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step9_data = await pyRun(`
import json, numpy as np
from sonar.waveform import lfm_chirp
from sonar.targets import Target, echo
from sonar.noise import white_noise, noise_sigma_for_snr
from sonar.matched_filter import matched_filter
from sonar.range_doppler import range_doppler_map, doppler_axis_velocity
from sonar import C_WATER
Fs = max(4 * B, 2 * fc)
_, s = lfm_chirp(B, tau, Fs)
n_range = int(2.0 * 1.5 * R0 / C_WATER * Fs) + s.size + 64
N_pulse_int = int(N_pulse)
tgt = Target(R0_m=R0, v_mps=v, TS_dB=-15.0)
pulses = np.empty((N_pulse_int, n_range), dtype=np.complex128)
rng = np.random.default_rng(5)
sig_pwr_ref = None
for k in range(N_pulse_int):
    y_e = echo(s, Fs, fc, tgt, SL_dB=200.0, n_out=n_range)
    if sig_pwr_ref is None:
        sig_pwr_ref = np.mean(np.abs(y_e[y_e != 0]) ** 2) if np.any(y_e != 0) else 1.0
    sigma = noise_sigma_for_snr(sig_pwr_ref, snr_in_dB)
    nz = white_noise(n_range, sigma, rng=rng)
    phase = np.exp(-2j * np.pi * (2 * v * fc / C_WATER) * k * PRI)
    pulses[k, :] = matched_filter(y_e * phase + nz, s)
rd_dB, fd = range_doppler_map(pulses, PRI, window=True)
v_axis = doppler_axis_velocity(fd, fc)
r_axis = np.arange(n_range) / Fs * C_WATER / 2.0
# downsample the range axis for the heatmap payload
step = max(1, r_axis.size // 1024)
r_ax_ds = r_axis[::step]
rd_dB_ds = rd_dB[:, ::step]
json.dumps({
  "r": r_ax_ds.tolist(), "v": v_axis.tolist(),
  "z": rd_dB_ds.tolist(), "zmax": float(rd_dB_ds.max()),
  "R0": float(R0), "v_target": float(v),
  "N_pulse": int(N_pulse_int), "PRI_ms": float(PRI) * 1e3,
})
`, {B, tau, fc, R0, v, snr_in_dB, N_pulse, PRI})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step9_plot = {
  const d = step9_data;
  const div = html`<div style="width:100%;height:380px"></div>`;
  Plotly.newPlot(div, [{
    type: "heatmap",
    z: d.z, x: d.r, y: d.v,
    colorscale: "Viridis",
    zmin: d.zmax - 60, zmax: d.zmax,
    colorbar: {title: "dB", thickness: 10},
    hovertemplate: "range %{x:.1f} m<br>v %{y:.2f} m/s<br>%{z:.1f} dB<extra></extra>",
  }, {
    type: "scatter", mode: "markers",
    x: [d.R0], y: [d.v_target],
    marker: {size: 14, color: "rgba(0,0,0,0)",
             line: {color: "red", width: 1.5}, symbol: "circle"},
    name: "true target cell",
    hovertemplate: "true target<br>R₀ %{x:.0f} m, v %{y:.2f} m/s<extra></extra>",
  }], {
    margin: {l: 60, r: 10, t: 36, b: 42},
    xaxis: {title: "range (m)"},
    yaxis: {title: "radial velocity (m/s)"},
    title: {text: `range–Doppler map, N_pulse = ${d.N_pulse}, PRI = ${d.PRI_ms.toFixed(2)} ms`,
            font: {size: 13}},
    showlegend: false,
  }, {displayModeBar: true, displaylogo: false, responsive: true,
     modeBarButtonsToRemove: ["select2d","lasso2d","toggleSpikelines"]});
  return div;
}
```

## Step 10 — CA-CFAR detection

Cell-averaging CFAR estimates the local noise power from a ring of
training cells (with a small guard band around the cell under test),
multiplies by $\alpha = N\bigl(P_\text{fa}^{-1/N}-1\bigr)$, and thresholds.
We run it across the range axis of the peak Doppler bin and annotate
detections on the RD map.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step10_data = await pyRun(`
import json, numpy as np
from sonar.waveform import lfm_chirp
from sonar.targets import Target, echo
from sonar.noise import white_noise, noise_sigma_for_snr
from sonar.matched_filter import matched_filter
from sonar.range_doppler import range_doppler_map, doppler_axis_velocity
from sonar.detection import ca_cfar_threshold, detect, roc_curve
from sonar import C_WATER
Fs = max(4 * B, 2 * fc)
_, s = lfm_chirp(B, tau, Fs)
n_range = int(2.0 * 1.5 * R0 / C_WATER * Fs) + s.size + 64
N_pulse_int = int(N_pulse)
tgt = Target(R0_m=R0, v_mps=v, TS_dB=-15.0)
pulses = np.empty((N_pulse_int, n_range), dtype=np.complex128)
rng = np.random.default_rng(6)
sig_pwr_ref = None
for k in range(N_pulse_int):
    y_e = echo(s, Fs, fc, tgt, SL_dB=200.0, n_out=n_range)
    if sig_pwr_ref is None:
        sig_pwr_ref = np.mean(np.abs(y_e[y_e != 0]) ** 2) if np.any(y_e != 0) else 1.0
    sigma = noise_sigma_for_snr(sig_pwr_ref, snr_in_dB)
    nz = white_noise(n_range, sigma, rng=rng)
    phase = np.exp(-2j * np.pi * (2 * v * fc / C_WATER) * k * PRI)
    pulses[k, :] = matched_filter(y_e * phase + nz, s)
rd_dB, fd = range_doppler_map(pulses, PRI, window=True)
v_axis = doppler_axis_velocity(fd, fc)
r_axis = np.arange(n_range) / Fs * C_WATER / 2.0
rd_pwr = 10.0 ** (rd_dB / 10.0)
det_mask = np.zeros_like(rd_pwr, dtype=bool)
for iv in range(rd_pwr.shape[0]):
    th, _ = ca_cfar_threshold(rd_pwr[iv, :], train=16, guard=2, Pfa=Pfa)
    det_mask[iv, :] = detect(rd_pwr[iv, :], th)
step = max(1, r_axis.size // 1024)
r_ds = r_axis[::step]
rd_dB_ds = rd_dB[:, ::step]
det_iv, det_ir = np.nonzero(det_mask[:, ::step])
snr_dB_range = np.linspace(-20, 20, 201)
_, pd = roc_curve(snr_dB_range, n_pulses=N_pulse_int)
json.dumps({
  "r": r_ds.tolist(), "v": v_axis.tolist(),
  "z": rd_dB_ds.tolist(), "zmax": float(rd_dB_ds.max()),
  "det_r": r_ds[det_ir].tolist(),
  "det_v": v_axis[det_iv].tolist(),
  "R0": float(R0), "v_target": float(v),
  "N_pulse": int(N_pulse_int),
  "Pfa": float(Pfa),
  "snr_grid": snr_dB_range.tolist(), "pd": pd.tolist(),
  "snr_in_dB": float(snr_in_dB),
})
`, {B, tau, fc, R0, v, snr_in_dB, N_pulse, PRI, Pfa})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step10_rdmap = {
  const d = step10_data;
  const div = html`<div style="width:100%;height:360px"></div>`;
  Plotly.newPlot(div, [{
    type: "heatmap",
    z: d.z, x: d.r, y: d.v,
    colorscale: "Viridis",
    zmin: d.zmax - 60, zmax: d.zmax,
    colorbar: {title: "dB", thickness: 10},
    hovertemplate: "range %{x:.1f} m<br>v %{y:.2f} m/s<br>%{z:.1f} dB<extra></extra>",
  }, {
    type: "scatter", mode: "markers",
    x: d.det_r, y: d.det_v,
    marker: {symbol: "x", color: "red", size: 7, line: {width: 1.2}},
    name: `CFAR hits (Pfa=${d.Pfa.toExponential(0)})`,
    hovertemplate: "CFAR hit<br>range %{x:.1f} m, v %{y:.2f} m/s<extra></extra>",
  }, {
    type: "scatter", mode: "markers",
    x: [d.R0], y: [d.v_target],
    marker: {symbol: "circle-open", color: "white", size: 14, line: {color: "white", width: 1.2}},
    name: "true target",
    hovertemplate: "true target<br>R₀ %{x:.0f} m, v %{y:.2f} m/s<extra></extra>",
  }], {
    margin: {l: 60, r: 10, t: 36, b: 42},
    xaxis: {title: "range (m)"},
    yaxis: {title: "radial velocity (m/s)"},
    title: {text: "RD map + CFAR detections", font: {size: 13}},
    legend: {x: 0.99, y: 0.99, xanchor: "right", yanchor: "top", bgcolor: "rgba(255,255,255,0.6)"},
  }, {displayModeBar: true, displaylogo: false, responsive: true,
     modeBarButtonsToRemove: ["select2d","lasso2d","toggleSpikelines"]});
  return div;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
step10_roc = {
  const d = step10_data;
  const pts = d.snr_grid.map((snr, i) => ({snr, pd: Math.max(d.pd[i], 1e-6)}));
  return Plot.plot({
    width: 720, height: 260,
    marginLeft: 60, marginBottom: 42,
    x: {label: "input SNR (dB)", grid: true},
    y: {label: "Pd", type: "log", grid: true, domain: [1e-3, 1.1]},
    marks: [
      Plot.line(pts, {x: "snr", y: "pd", stroke: "steelblue", strokeWidth: 1.4}),
      Plot.ruleX([d.snr_in_dB], {stroke: "#c0392b", strokeDasharray: "4 3"}),
      Plot.text([{x: d.snr_in_dB, y: 1}],
        {x: "x", y: "y", text: [`current SNR = ${d.snr_in_dB.toFixed(0)} dB`],
         fill: "#c0392b", dx: 4, textAnchor: "start", fontSize: 10}),
    ],
    caption: `ROC, Pfa = ${d.Pfa.toExponential(0)}, N_pulse = ${d.N_pulse}`,
  });
}
```

## How this works {.unnumbered}

A quick tour of the stack, because the *mechanism* is the actual point of
this document:

- **[Quarto](https://quarto.org)** authors the page. The `.qmd` source
  declares ten sections, eight interactive knobs, and the OJS + pyodide
  cells that produce each figure.
- **[Pyodide](https://pyodide.org)** is CPython 3.12 compiled to
  WebAssembly. It runs in a worker thread, ships with NumPy/SciPy as
  precompiled wheels, and exposes the `pyodide.runPythonAsync(...)`
  API that our OJS cells call to do the DSP math. The whole runtime is
  self-hosted under `./pyodide/` so no CDN request is needed at runtime.
- **[OJS](https://quarto.org/docs/interactive/ojs/)** (Observable JS)
  owns the reactive graph. A lightweight `pyRun` helper wraps each
  Python call so (a) slider values are piped into Pyodide globals,
  (b) calls are serialised onto a single promise queue, and (c) the
  sticky status pill tracks per-batch progress.
- **Plotting is now client-side.** Line, scatter and ROC figures are
  drawn with [Observable Plot](https://observablehq.com/plot); the two
  range–Doppler heatmaps use [Plotly](https://plotly.com/javascript/)
  (also self-hosted, no CDN). Python returns bare arrays as JSON, JS
  renders, and interaction features (hover tooltips, box-zoom, pan,
  legend toggle) come for free. The **Apply matched filter** toggle in
  particular flips in JS without a Python round-trip because both
  branches (bypass and MF output) are computed once in the data cell.
- **Our DSP code** lives in the `sonar/` Python package and is imported
  by both the browser-side Pyodide runtime and the local `pytest` suite
  that verifies the analytical invariants (chirp instantaneous frequency,
  MF gain, MF delay localisation, Doppler-bin localisation, and
  CA-CFAR's empirical $P_\text{fa}$).

No servers, no notebooks, no kernels, no CDN. The `index.html` file you
are reading is fully self-contained: the Pyodide runtime + scientific
wheels + Plotly are all served from the same origin as the page. After
the initial ~50 MB warm-up (cached on first visit), the report runs
fully offline.

### Repro in 30 seconds {.unnumbered}

```bash
git clone <this repo> && cd sonar-demo
pip install -e .[dev]           # installs the sonar package + pytest
pytest                          # 5 analytical checks
quarto add r-wasm/quarto-live   # if not already present
quarto render report.qmd        # writes _site/report.html
```

Host `_site/report.html` wherever you like (GitHub Pages, Netlify, S3,
Quarto Pub, an HTTP server on a laptop, `file://` from disk). After the
initial Pyodide download there are no network calls — the report runs
offline.
